prompt: How do we define training hard enough for typical lifters trying to get stronger or improve physique?
expected_output: They framed this around progressive overload and recoverability, not constant all-out effort. Eric said many productive sets can live around 0-4 reps in reserve (RIR), often starting farther from failure and moving closer across a block. They also discussed volume as a lever: if progress stalls and deloads are almost never needed, you may be under-dosing training stress. Greg added practical heuristics that repeatedly needing deloads every ~4-8 weeks can indicate productive difficulty, whereas crashing every 1-2 weeks suggests too much. Their short version was 'harder than last time,' while still preserving safety and long-term consistency.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on how hard to train
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer includes actionable practical recommendations consistent with the hosts' advice
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
